text:
Powered byOutside

(Photo:  ANP Sport via Getty Images)

Mathieu van der Poelwill be chasingOlympicgold this summer in Paris, but it won’t be in mountain biking.



The Alpecin-Deceuninck superstar confirmed Wednesday he will not be racing off-road in the Paris Olympic Games, and instead will fully commit to the Olympic road race as well as return to theTour de France.

“The most logical choice. It ensures that I have a little more time and thus can prepare longer and better,” Van der Poel said Wednesday.

Van der Poel already had a start position for the Olympic mountain bike race, and was keen to make up for his costly crash that knocked him out of the running in the Tokyo Olympic Games in 2021.

Yet once he saw a favorable road race course in and around Paris, he made the call that the best chance for success for an elusive Olympic gold medal would be focusing 100 percent for the road race.

“Which made for a difficult puzzle to put together,” Van der Poel said in a team statement Wednesday. “Just the road race? Only mountain biking? Or both? Whether or not in combination with the Tour de France, which I personally consider the best possible preparation for the Games.”

The decision comes after months of trying to decide how to manage his training and focus in what’s been a highly successful season racing in the world champion’s rainbow jersey.

Van der Poel lit up the spring classics, with huge wins at E3 Saxo Classic, Tour of Flanders and Paris-Roubaix, a podium at Liège-Bastogne-Liège, as well as top-10s at Milan-San Remo and Gent-Wevelgem.

Van der Poel already secured a spot on the Dutch mountain bike team, but took a considered decision that his best chances to win Olympic gold in Paris would be in the elite men’s road race.

“Let’s say I chose the most logical thing,” he said. “My first half of the season was quite long. First the cyclocross season. After a short break, I resumed training in function of the spring season and I continued that campaign up to Liège-Bastogne-Liège. Now, if I want to focus fully on mountain biking and be perfectly prepared, I have to start as early as next weekend in Nove Mesto, the Czech manche of the MTB World Cup.

“So, in consultation with the team, I opted for a slightly longer rest period, after which I can build up to the Tour de France and the Olympic road race without time pressure.

“The fact that I am currently riding in the rainbow jersey has indirectly played a bit of a role. It’s a special year. As world champion I like to ride in that jersey as much as possible. So I also prefer not to miss the Tour de France. And that proved to be a good preparation last year, then towards the World Championships.”

Van der Poel is one of the most prolific, multi-disciplined riders of the modern era. A six-time world champion in cyclocross, Van der Poel dreamed of becoming an Olympic champion in mountain biking.

He didn’t close the door entirely, and said he might make another push in the next Olympic cycle.

“Who knows what’s still possible in 2028 in Los Angeles? That’s still a long time away, but I’ll be in a different phase of my career then,” he said. “Maybe then I can put everything on that mountain biking. This year the combination is just too difficult.”

Van der Poel was a top favorite for gold in the Tokyo Olympic Games, contested in 2021 after a year-long delay due to COVID-19.

He crashed out, however, after misjudging a drop, and fell heavily on a bike that resulted in an injury that plagued him across the 2022 racing season.

After a short break, including a vacation in UAE, Van der Poel resumed training in Spain.He won’t race before returning to the Tour, where he will share co-leader duties with Jasper Philipsen.

“I leave a week later than the rest of our Tour squad on altitude training to La Plagne. After that, I’ll work through some individual training sessions until the start of the Tour in Florence,” he said. “In the meantime we have gained enough experience to know that once in competition, I quickly find my form.

“In the Tour, I hope to assist teammate Jasper Philipsen again in his hunt for stage wins and a possible green jersey. And the goal is to win a stage myself this year. Last year I got sick when there were a few stages where I had chances. Hopefully I will be spared from that in this edition.”

🇳🇱 Mathieu van der Poel will ride the 🇫🇷 Tour de France and the Road Race at the Olympics in 🇫🇷 Paris 2024 this summer. No MTB at the Olympics."And the mountain biking? Who knows what's still possible in 2028 in Los Angeles? This year the combination is just too difficult."pic.twitter.com/jsqiZZ9oeJ
title: Van der Poel to Skip Mountain Biking in Paris, Will Commit to Road
author: Andrew Hood
description: Van der Poel continues with select calendar, but without the Olympic mountain bike race. The Tour de France and Olympic road race up next.
